## 🗂️ Recall: Formula Card (Your formulas here...) ## 📝 Practice Problems ::: {.cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Polar plot](index_files/figure-pdf/cell-2-output-1.pdf){} ::: ::: ## 📝 Practice Gallery ::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::: {.cell execution_count=2} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Polar plot](index_files/figure-pdf/cell-3-output-1.pdf){} ::: ::: ### 🟡 Hard ### 🔴 Challenging ::: {.callout-important .difficulty-challenging icon="false"} ## [CHALLENGING]{.badge} Exercise Prove that if $a + b = c$, then $(a+b)^2 = c^2$. --- **Solution:** Since $a+b$ is identical to $c$, squaring both sides of the equality $a+b = c$ directly yields $(a+b)^2 = c^2$ by the substitution property. ::: :::
